CCP 170.6 TO THE HONORABLE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK J. RUMSEY, NOT REPORTED.
MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-09-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT LSL PROPERTIES, B14 DE, LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR SFRO AND UCL VIOLATION. THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED AS TO THE NAMED PLAINTIFFS. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS PLAINTIFFS HAVE AN ADEQUATE REMEDY AT LAW. DEMURRER TO CLASS ACTION ALLEGATIONS IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE A VIABLE CLASS CLAIM IN LIGHT OF THE ISSUES RAISED BY THE DEMURRER (INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO NUMEROSITY AND COMMONALITY) AND THE RELIEF SOUGHT IN THE PROPERLY ALLEGED CAUSES OF ACTION. (COMPARE PUTATIVE CLASS PARAGRAPH 88 AND PARAGRAPHS 103, 115). PLAINTIFF HAS 30 DAYS TO AMEND. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

Demurrer To Second Amended Class Action Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-19-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Brent D Meyer of the law offices of Meyer Law Group LLP (415.765.1588) appears as counsel for plaintiffs/opposing parties

Attorney, Brett E. Bitzer of the law offices of Callahan Thompson Sherman & Caudill LLP (949.261.2872) appears as counsel for plaintiffs/opposing parties

Attorney, Kyle A. Withers of the law offices of Lubin Olson & Niewiadomski LLP (415.981.0550) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendant LSL Properties, B14 DE, LLC's Demurrer to Second Amended Class Action Complaint, as to the class allegations is OVERRULED. Plaintiffs Andrew Kane, Tera Anderson, Ileana Falticeni, and Fifth Age Consulting, Inc. have adequately alleged class allegations as to both the first cause of action for violation of the San Francisco Rent Ordinance and the second cause of action for violation of the Unfair Competition Law. "[W]hen class certification is challenged by demurrer, the trial court must determine whether there is a reasonable possibility plaintiffs can plead a prima facie community of interest among class members. If there is a reasonable possibility the plaintiffs can plead a prima facie community of interest among class members, the preferred course is to defer decision on the propriety of the class action until an evidentiary hearing has been held on the appropriateness of class litigation." (Tucker v. Pacific Bell Mobil Services (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 201, 215 (citations omitted).) LSL's argument that class treatment is inappropriate because the issue whether each class member was a "tenant in occupancy" requires extensive individual determinations is not amenable to resolution on a demurrer and must await an evidentiary hearing. "[I]ndividual issues do not render class certification inappropriate so long as such issues may be effectively managed." (Sav-On Drug Stores, Inc. v. Superior Court (2004) 34 Cal.4th 319, 334.) Plaintiffs' proposals for subclasses and a rubric or survey are reasonably possible solutions for the effective management of the individual issues inherent in the determination of whether class members were tenants in occupancy. Based on plaintiffs' list of alleged common questions of law and fact (SAC 100), it is reasonably possible that common questions (especially those relating to defendants' alleged "scheme") predominate over individualized questions. Nor has LSL shown that the allegations in the second amended complaint are outside the scope of reasonably possibly class certification because of the lack of either numerosity or superiority of class treatment. Because there is a reasonable possibility plaintiffs will be able to certify their proposed class, a decision on the propriety of the class action must await an evidentiary hearing. Plaintiffs' objections to LSL's request for judicial notice are sustained. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-09-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Kyle Withers, 415-981-0550 for Defendant/Moving party LSL Properties, B14 DE, LLC.
Brent Meyer, 415--765-1588 for Plaintiff/Opposing Andrew Kane.

Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT LSL PROPERTIES, B14 DE, LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is overruled as to the causes of action for SFRO and UCL violation. These causes of action are sufficiently alleged as to the named Plaintiffs. Demurrer to the cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained without leave to amend as plaintiffs have an adequate remedy at law. Demurrer to class action allegations is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege a viable class claim in light of the issues raised by the demurrer (including but not limited to numerosity and commonality) and the relief sought in the properly alleged causes of action. (Compare putative class paragraph 88 and paragraphs 103, 115). Plaintiff has 30 days to amend. Order signed in open Court. Judge: A. James Robertson II Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
